android: force OpenGL RHI + proper NullBackend to fix SIGABRT

- Force QSGRendererInterface::OpenGLRhi before QGuiApplication init.
  Qt 6.9 defaults to Vulkan on Android, which conflicts with Android 16
  HWUI Vulkan renderer on Samsung Xclipse 540, causing SIGABRT in
  hwuiTask0 ~500ms after startup.

- Replace plain QObject null backend with NullBackend class that
  exposes all Q_PROPERTYs (connected, voltage, current, etc.) with
  safe defaults. This prevents QML TypeError crashes when QML bindings
  evaluate before the user connects.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Remove forced toolchain pin from go.mod

Previously, go.mod forced all users and CI to use exactly go1.24.13.
This was unnecessarily restrictive.

The actual minimum Go version required is 1.24.0 (determined by the
dependency on github.com/hajimehoshi/ebiten/v2 v2.9.9). By removing
the toolchain line, users can now use any Go 1.24+ toolchain.

Also cleaned up an unused indirect dependency on opd-ai/wain that was
removed by go mod tidy.

Fixes: PLAN.md item 11 (6b)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
[Tests][UserEvents] Fix tracee discovery race and improve failure diagnostics (#125345)

https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123442

More diagnostics and better tracee timing in response to the failures
described in
https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/123442#issuecomment-4025436022

Wait for "Recording started" instead of fixed delay. The 300ms fixed
sleep before launching the tracee was insufficient on ARM64 CI where
record-trace's startup (proc scan + ring buffer setup) took ~1845ms. I
believe the tracee would start in the dead zone between the proc scan
and ring buffer enable, so record-trace never discovered it via mmap
events. Replace the sleep with a ManualResetEventSlim that gates on
record-trace's "Recording started" stdout message, which is printed
after PerfSession::enable succeeds.

Improve failure diagnostics
- Add --log-filter to capture debug-level record-trace diagnostics (mmap
discovery, IPC enablement, nettrace event flushing) while suppressing
noisy modules. The log is written to a file and uploaded to Helix only
on failure.
- Add DumpTraceeEvents to log a summary of all events from the tracee
PID in the nettrace on validation failure, to distinguish "event not
captured" from "event captured with unexpected metadata."
- Add tracee-side console messages confirming event emission completed,
and log the tracee exit code.

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Redirect build caches to native filesystem for v9fs compat

The bind-mounted /copilot-home uses v9fs which doesn't support
atomic rename(). Zig and Cargo builds fail with AccessDenied.
Fix by setting ZIG_LOCAL_CACHE_DIR, ZIG_GLOBAL_CACHE_DIR, and
CARGO_TARGET_DIR to paths on the native container filesystem.

commit.message:
Fix voice loop stopping between form inputs

- Remove duplicate StartVoiceLoop() from AskCurrentFieldAsync that was
  spawning competing loops on each field advance
- AskCurrentFieldAsync now only speaks the question; voice loop resumes
  listening automatically when SpeakAsync returns
- OnAppearing properly sequences: speak first question → start single loop

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(mc): fix Y-boundary layer shift — surface-at-top detection by material

The previous has_air scan was wrong: when the terrain surface genuinely
sits at the very top of a chunk (surface_voxel_y = max_v.y - 1), all
voxels WITHIN the chunk below it are solid STONE/DIRT — there is no AIR
because the AIR is in the +Y chunk above.  has_air returned false, so
the column was classified as all-solid → surface_y = max_v.y + 2.0 →
Y=44841 rendered nothing for that column.  The +Y chunk (Y=44842) saw
it as all-air (surface below) and also rendered nothing.  Result: the
entire surface of that column was MISSING from both chunks, creating the
'3D printer layer shift' / horizontal cut visible at ~59m.

Fix: check the MATERIAL of the topmost voxel (O(1) instead of O(200)):
  • GRASS / ASPHALT / CONCRETE / WATER → genuine surface at chunk top
    → surface_y = (max_v.y - 1) + 0.5  (render correctly)
  • DIRT / STONE / GRAVEL / anything else → all-solid, terrain above
    → surface_y = max_v.y + 2.0  (no phantom, noise margin ≥ 1.5)

This also removes the O(200) has_air column scan, saving ~180K voxel
reads per chunk load.

Bump: v0.1.73 → v0.1.74

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

[tests] Fix crash in BitmapContextTest.CreateAdaptive_2 by computing correct buffer size. (#24862)

The hardcoded 512-byte rendering buffer was far too small for a 256x256
adaptive bitmap context. Replace with the correct size computed from the
adaptive parameters (AlignedBytesPerRow * Height) to avoid SIGSEGV in
CGBitmapContextCreateImage when it tries to read beyond the buffer.

commit.message:
fix: oh-my-posh install - duplicate label, font --user flag, CONFIG NOT FOUND

Three bugs fixed:

1. Duplicate 'Oh My Posh...' label: line 426 wrote the label, then
   Install-WingetApp wrote it again. Fixed by removing the outer Write-Host
   and handling already-installed case directly without going through the helper.

2. CascadiaCode font install: --user flag does not exist in oh-my-posh 29.x.
   Removed the --user attempt and use LASTEXITCODE to detect success instead
   of string-matching against progress-bar-heavy output.

3. CONFIG NOT FOUND on new machine: 'oh-my-posh get shell-themes-path' is
   not a valid subcommand (returns exit code 70). POSH_THEMES_PATH is empty
   because the Store/WinGet version of oh-my-posh does not set it.
   Fix: download 7 curated themes to ~\.config\oh-my-posh\ at install time
   (skipping any already present), create current-theme.txt if missing, and
   update the profile block to use ~\.config\oh-my-posh with a proper guard
   and Set-Theme helper function for theme switching.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix false SENSOR_TAMPERING alerts on generator/transfer buses

for generator buses. Now only checks buses with meterCount > 0.

commit.message:
feat: browser tool gap-fill — 93 new MCP actions for full CLI parity

Add comprehensive gap-fill handlers to the browser MCP tool:
- HAR: start/drain/export (3 actions)
- Network log: start/drain/summary/stop/export (5 actions)
- WebSocket: start/drain/export/connections (4 actions)
- Cookies file I/O: export/import/delete (3 actions)
- Cookie jar: export/import/clear (3 actions)
- Domain blocking: block/block_category/unblock/stats/list/categories (6 actions)
- Proxy: create_pool/chrome_args/next (3 actions)
- Proxy health: check/check_all/rank/filter (4 actions)
- Request execution: execute/batch (2 actions)
- Fingerprint: apply/detect (2 actions)
- Coverage: js_start/js_stop/css_start/css_report (4 actions)
- Performance: trace_start/trace_stop/metrics/timing/resources (5 actions)
- Workers: list/unregister/info (3 actions)
- Iframes: list/eval/content (3 actions)
- Page watcher: start/drain/stop/state (4 actions)
- Accessibility: tree/element/audit (3 actions)
- Print/PDF: pdf/metrics (2 actions)
- Benchmarks: run/report (2 actions)
- Window: new (1 action)
- Rate limiting: set/stats/reset (3 actions)
- Diff: snapshot/screenshot/url (3 actions)
- Downloads: fetch/clear (2 actions)
- Screenshot diff: compare/regression (2 actions)
- Dialog extended: set_handler/accept/dismiss/history/clear (5 actions)
- Network block: block by type (1 action)
- Highlight: selector (1 action)

All handlers delegate to onecrawl_cdp functions (real CDP, no stubs).
574 tests pass, zero warnings.

commit.message:
Rewrite LogDrinkPage as client-side state machine

Replace unreliable AI-driven form walkthrough with deterministic state machine.
- Each field (bourbon→sweetener→bitters→garnish→ice→rating→notes) has a
  hardcoded question and advances on user answer
- Questions detected client-side and sent to AI WITHOUT tools/history
- 'skip' keeps default values, 'go back' navigates away
- Auto-saves after all fields completed
- AI only used for answering questions, not driving form flow
- Fixes: repeated questions, stuck on FillDrinkForm, context overflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(web): convert all page SEO metadata from hardcoded to CMS-driven

Convert static `export const metadata` to dynamic `generateMetadata()`
across all pages to pull SEO metadata from Sanity CMS.

Changes:
- Layout: uses getSiteSettings() for site-wide defaults with fallbacks
- Homepage: generateMetadata via getPageContent('homepage')
- About, Pricing, Features, Contact, Blog, Changelog: generateMetadata
  via getPageContent() with per-page slug and hardcoded fallbacks
- Privacy, Terms: generateMetadata via getPagePost() with fallbacks

Blog post detail and changelog detail pages already had dynamic
generateMetadata and are unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Deprecate Button.SetText in favor of SetLabel

Button widget had both SetLabel and SetText methods performing identical
operations. SetLabel is semantically correct as it matches the internal
'label' field. SetText has been deprecated with a clear migration path.

Fixes: AUDIT-2026-03-10.md LOW priority finding
- Added deprecation notice to SetText godoc
- Verified with 'go doc Button.SetText'
- No existing usage found in codebase
- Build and vet pass successfully

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Restore cwd rewrite in relay — required for exec mode

Even exec mode sends session/new with the client's local cwd, which
the Linux container rejects as non-absolute. The relay must rewrite
it to the container path.

commit.message:
Fix symlink validation tests on Windows (#338)

## Problem

Two tests in `InputValidationTests` were failing on Windows:
- `ValidateImagePath_SymlinkOutsideImagesDir_ReturnsNotAllowed`
- `ValidateImagePath_DirectorySymlinkBypass_ReturnsNotAllowed`

Both expected `"Path not allowed"` but got `"Unsupported file type"`.

## Root Cause

The tests used Unix-style absolute paths (`/etc/passwd`, `/etc`) as
symlink targets. On Windows, these paths lack a drive letter, so
`ResolveLinkTarget` resolves them **relative to the symlink's parent
directory**. This caused the resolved path to land *inside* the images
directory, bypassing the containment check and falling through to the
file-extension validation instead.

## Fix

Replaced hardcoded Unix paths with platform-appropriate fully-qualified
absolute paths using `Path.GetTempPath()`, which is guaranteed to be
outside the images directory on all platforms.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Roadmap: reopen 9 issues, move XPC to Tier 2 alongside VM pipeline

Reopened: #23 (XPC broker — defense-in-depth for multi-VM),
#41, #107, #108 (hardware/scheduling), #91 (VS Code ext),
#205 (code intelligence tracking), #246 (doc parsing),
#249, #275 (RAG CSS/Tailwind)

XPC now in Tier 2: when multiple VMs hit the host MCP server
concurrently, XPC broker provides per-caller identity checks
so VM1 can't invoke tools scoped to VM2.

36 open issues, 307 closed. Only #43/#44/#109 remain closed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add install_docker() and ensure_brew(), align install methods with CI

- install_docker(): macOS uses brew --cask docker (+ opens Docker Desktop),
  Ubuntu uses official docker-ce apt repo
- ensure_brew(): shared Homebrew check used by all macOS installers
- All install_* functions now match the exact commands proven green in CI
- Docker auto-installed if missing instead of failing with error

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

bench: run incremental GC between benchmark samples (#1050)

Some benchmarks (e.g. gp_lml) allocate ~500 KiB per call. With
evals=1 and no GC between samples, garbage accumulates until the GC
fires mid-sample, inflating that sample's time by 2-3x or more.

Changing the Chairmarks teardown from `_ -> true` to `_ -> GC.gc(false)`
runs an incremental GC between each sample, outside the timed region,
keeping the heap clean and preventing GC from interrupting evaluations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move System.* package versions to Version.Details.xml for source-build (#19397)

The PR dotnet/fsharp#19021 moved System.Collections.Immutable,
System.Composition, System.Diagnostics.DiagnosticSource, and
System.Reflection.Metadata from Version.Details.xml to hardcoded
properties in Versions.props. This causes prebuilt failures in
source-build because these packages are no longer tracked as
dependencies from dotnet/runtime.

Move them back to Version.Details.xml (with empty Sha, matching the
pattern used by dotnet/msbuild) and auto-generated Version.Details.props,
removing the hardcoded SystemPackagesVersion from Versions.props.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix Function App startup failure: switch to Oryx remote build for Python dependencies (#8)

* Initial plan

* fix: switch to Oryx remote build to resolve Python binary extension version mismatch

The deploy workflow was pre-installing packages on the CI runner (Python 3.11)
and deploying them with scm-do-build-during-deployment=false. When the Function
App runtime uses a different Python version (e.g. 3.13), binary C extensions
like _cffi_backend (from cffi/cryptography used by azure-identity) fail to load.

Changes:
- deploy-function.yml: install deps locally for tests only (no --target);
  set scm-do-build-during-deployment=true so Azure Oryx builds packages on
  the actual runtime environment
- function-app.bicep: add SCM_DO_BUILD_DURING_DEPLOYMENT=1 and
  ENABLE_ORYX_BUILD=true app settings to ensure the Oryx pipeline runs

fix: require SECRET_TOKEN at startup to prevent auth bypass (#476)

* fix: require SECRET_TOKEN at startup to prevent auth bypass

The original verify_token() allowed None == None when SECRET_TOKEN was
unset, silently disabling authentication.  The server now refuses to
start without SECRET_TOKEN configured, and validate_user() accepts the
static token via constant-time comparison (hmac.compare_digest) as an
alternative to DB-backed OAuth tokens.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: add pylint disable for wrong-import-position in conftest.py

The imports must come after os.environ.setdefault() for SECRET_TOKEN,
which is intentionally non-standard. Suppress the C0413 warning.

commit.message:
v0.3.9: fix Wi-Fi hotspot not appearing on first boot

Three root causes found by inspecting v0.3.8 image via Parallels + loop-mount:

1. wpa_supplicant was fighting hostapd for wlan0
   - Bookworm Lite enables wpa_supplicant by default (Wi-Fi client mode)
   - Removed multi-user.target.wants/wpa_supplicant.service symlink directly
     in chroot (systemctl disable is unreliable without running D-Bus)

2. userconf.txt was written with no password hash (interactive login prompt)
   - openssl passwd -6 -stdin outputs nothing on Linux — not portable
   - Fixed: openssl passwd -6 'logfalcon' (direct arg, works everywhere)
   - Fixed: userconfig.service symlink removed directly (was silently failing
     via 'systemctl disable' in chroot)

3. dnsmasq starts before wlan0 is configured (bind-interfaces race)
   - Added systemd overrides for dnsmasq and hostapd:
     After=sys-subsystem-net-devices-wlan0.device, Restart=on-failure

Also adds scripts/inspect-image.sh: mounts any .img/.img.xz via Parallels
or Docker and audits all hotspot configs, services, binary, and known issues.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Triage: close 12 issues, reorganize roadmap into 7 tiers

Closed as superseded/premature/out-of-scope:
- #23 (XPC broker → VM isolation), #41 (budget scheduler),
  #43/#44 (replay/quorum → approval gates), #107/#108 (GPU/ANE),
  #109 (voice notifications), #91 (VS Code ext), #205 (tracking done),
  #246 (document parsing), #249/#275 (CSS/Tailwind lint)

Roadmap now organized as:
- Tier 1: Polish & Harden (#352, #336, #335, #277)
- Tier 2: VM Pipeline (#312→#310)
- Tier 3: P8 Job Infra (#347→#351)
- Tier 4: P9 Enterprise PR (#353→#356)
- Tier 5: RAG (#274, #276)
- Tier 6: Xcode MCP (#363→#365)
- Tier 7: Aspirational (#35, #36, #193)

27 open issues remain, down from 39.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: toml 1.0 compat + crate_deps pipeline tests; fix approx PI lint

Merges two bug fixes from dev:

1. fix(tests): replace approximate PI literal with std::f64::consts::PI
   Clippy's approx_constant lint (-D warnings) rejected 3.14 in
   verification_serde_test.rs, failing all three CI jobs.

2. fix(emit): use toml::from_str instead of .parse() for toml 1.0 compat
   toml 1.0 removed str.parse::<toml::Value>() for full TOML documents.
   all_crate_deps() silently returned empty, so every #[elicit_tool]
   was missing all dependencies, breaking all 32 elicit_server smoke tests.

Testing gap closed:
   - elicit_url/tests/emit_deps_test.rs: 3-stage dep pipeline tests
   - elicit_server/tests/emit_smoke_test.rs: 5 crate_deps pipeline tests

CI: all 3 platforms green (run 22890919752)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch npm publish to use shared npm-tokens variable group (#393)

* Switch npm publish to use shared npm-tokens variable group

Replace the btt-npm-publish-token service connection with the npm-tokens
variable group used by azure-pipelines-tasks-common-packages. This writes
a temporary .npmrc with the NPM_TOKEN env var and publishes directly,
matching the common-packages publish pattern.

Also switches GH_TOKEN to use gh-automation.token from the same group.

master and build reason is IndividualCI, BatchedCI, or Manual.

commit.message:
Add complete News Topic Classifier system

- data/sample_data.csv: 180 synthetic news articles (25-30 per category) across
  7 topics: sports, technology, politics, business, entertainment, health, science
- data/README.md: data methodology and web scraping documentation
- src/preprocess.py: TextPreprocessor with HTML/URL/stopword cleaning
- src/evaluate.py: confusion matrix plots, classification report, ModelEvaluator
- src/train_tfidf.py: TF-IDF pipeline (LogisticRegression/LinearSVC/RandomForest)
- src/train_transformer.py: custom PyTorch Transformer classifier from scratch
- src/scraper.py: BeautifulSoup4 RSS + article scraper with configurable limits
- api/main.py: FastAPI with /predict, /predict/batch, /health, /categories;
  CORS origins configurable via CORS_ORIGINS env var
- frontend/index.html + style.css + app.js: responsive SPA with score bars
- notebooks/analysis.ipynb: EDA, preprocessing demo, training, evaluation
- requirements.txt, .gitignore, run.sh

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Implement Tab navigation and keyboard focus management

Extends PublicWidget interface with SetFocused/IsFocused methods to enable
keyboard focus management. Implements automatic Tab/Shift+Tab navigation
through focusable widgets (Button, TextInput).

Changes:
- Add SetFocused/IsFocused to PublicWidget interface and BasePublicWidget
- Add Focusable interface with CanTakeFocus method
- Implement focus chain auto-building from widget tree in FocusManager
- Update EventDispatcher.dispatchKey to handle Tab/Shift+Tab navigation
- Add keyboard activation (Enter/Space) for focused Buttons
- Add visual focus indicators (borders) for Button and TextInput
- Remove duplicate focused field from TextInput (use BasePublicWidget's)
- Add comprehensive tests: TestFocusNavigation, TestFocusableInterface,
  TestKeyboardActivation, TestFocusChainAutoBuilding
- Update doc.go with focus management examples

Validation:
- GOOS=windows go build ./... passes
- GOOS=windows go vet ./... passes
- Complexity metrics within acceptable thresholds (dispatchKey: 8)

Resolves AUDIT-2026-03-10.md first finding.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
ci: add Ubuntu 22.04 workflow with Docker/MinIO integration tests

Ubuntu runners have Docker natively, so we can test the full flow:
- Python 3.10 via deadsnakes PPA
- mc binary install + alias/bucket creation
- minikube binary install
- MinIO containers (health check, bucket ops)
- Lithops config generation + backup logic
- venv creation + pip install

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add checksum consensus to ThreePhaseCommitMajority

- Add Checksum constant and rmChecksum variable for RM data state
- Add txConsensus (majority checksum) and txHealthy (matching RMs) per TX
- Compute consensus during lock acquisition; only healthy RMs can vote
- Add RMChecksumDiverge action to model data corruption
- Add RepairRMChecksum action to fix divergent RMs after commit
- Add ChecksumConsistency invariant (healthy locked RMs match consensus)
- Split Next into ProtocolNext (fair) and EnvNext (unfair) actions
  - Failures and divergence are adversarial, not required to stop
- Verified: 5.7M distinct states, depth 53, all properties pass

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
